findings.name: Side-tab accent border
findings.description: Thick colored border on one side of a card — the most recognizable tell of AI-generated UIs. Use a subtler accent or remove it entirely.

findings.name: Side-tab accent border
findings.description: Thick colored border on one side of a card — the most recognizable tell of AI-generated UIs. Use a subtler accent or remove it entirely.

findings.name: Side-tab accent border
findings.description: Thick colored border on one side of a card — the most recognizable tell of AI-generated UIs. Use a subtler accent or remove it entirely.

findings.name: Gradient text
findings.description: Gradient text is decorative rather than meaningful — a common AI tell, especially on headings and metrics. Use solid colors for text.

findings.name: Low contrast text
findings.description: Text does not meet WCAG AA contrast requirements (4.5:1 for body, 3:1 for large text). Increase the contrast between text and background.

findings.description: Text does not meet WCAG AA contrast requirements (4.5:1 for body, 3:1 for large text). Increase the contrast between text and background.

findings.name: Low contrast text
findings.description: Text does not meet WCAG AA contrast requirements (4.5:1 for body, 3:1 for large text). Increase the contrast between text and background.

findings.name: Hero eyebrow / pill chip
findings.description: A tiny uppercase letter-spaced label sitting immediately above an oversized hero headline — or the same shape rendered as a pill chip — is now the default AI SaaS hero. Drop the eyebrow, integrate the kicker into the headline, or run it as a navigation breadcrumb instead.
findings.snippet: eyebrow chip (tracked-caps) "Introducing Nimbus" above h1 "Supercharge your team with our enterprise-grade, world-class"

findings.name: Broken or placeholder image
findings.description: tags with empty src, missing src, or placeholder values ship as broken-image boxes. Use real images, generated assets, or remove the tag.

findings.name: Crushed letter spacing
findings.description: Letter-spacing pulled tighter than the point where characters keep their own shapes costs legibility. Tighten display type optically, not destructively.
findings.snippet: letter-spacing: -0.07em — "Supercharge your team with our enterpris"

findings.name: Tight line height
findings.description: Line height below 1.3x the font size makes multi-line text hard to read. Use 1.5 to 1.7 for body text so lines have room to breathe.

findings.name: Oversized hero headline
findings.description: A full-sentence headline set at display size ends up dominating the viewport, leaving no room for anything else above the fold. A punchy one- or two-word headline at that size is fine — the problem is a long headline blown up too large. Set long headlines smaller, or tighten the copy.
findings.snippet: 84px h1, 81 chars "Supercharge your team with our enterprise-grade, world-class"

findings.name: Overused font
findings.description: Inter, Roboto, Fraunces, Geist, Plus Jakarta Sans, and Space Grotesk are used on so many sites they no longer feel distinctive. Each new wave of AI-generated UIs converges on the same handful of faces. Choose a face that gives your interface personality.

findings.name: Single font for everything
findings.description: Only one font family is used for the entire page. Pair a distinctive display font with a refined body font to create typographic hierarchy.

findings.name: Skipped heading level
findings.description: Heading levels should not skip (e.g. h1 then h3 with no h2). Screen readers use heading hierarchy for navigation. Skipping levels breaks the document outline.
findings.snippet: <h1> "Supercharge your team with our enterprise-grade, world-class" followed by <h3> "Automate" (missing h2)

findings.name: AI color palette
findings.description: Purple/violet gradients and cyan-on-dark are the most recognizable tells of AI-generated UIs. Choose a distinctive, intentional palette.

findings.name: Gradient text
findings.description: Gradient text is decorative rather than meaningful — a common AI tell, especially on headings and metrics. Use solid colors for text.

findings.name: Dark mode with glowing accents
findings.description: Dark backgrounds with colored box-shadow glows are the default "cool" look of AI-generated UIs. Use subtle, purposeful lighting instead — or skip the dark theme entirely.

findings.name: Marketing buzzword
findings.description: Generic SaaS phrases (streamline / empower / supercharge / world-class / enterprise-grade / next-generation / cutting-edge / etc) are instant AI tells. Pick a specific verb and noun that says what the product literally does.
findings.snippet: 8 buzzword phrases: "cing Nimbus Supercharge your team with o"
